marketState: split signal: bullish medium-term fundamentals vs bearish short-term technicals
summary: NG front trades at $2.875, essentially at fair value (-$0.044 vs $2.919 fair) and about 6pp below its seasonal median for this time of year, while managed money is net-short at just the 10th percentile of the past 5 years - a classic crowded-short setup. Weather demand for days 8-16 is running +51.7% vs the 5-year average and above the historical max range, with the near-term trend also elevated (+28% vs 5y normal, still rising) - a bullish tailwind for balances heading into peak cooling season. Working against that: every technical timeframe from 5-minute to weekly is bearish with 50<200 EMA crosses, and this week's storage build beat forecast by +17 Bcf (bearish surprise) with inventories +183 Bcf above the 5-year average, a comfortable supply cushion. Seasonality is modestly supportive (69% of the past 16 years saw prices higher 1 and 4 weeks out, +3.7% typical move) but reliability is explicitly flagged Low (63pp spread), so it carries less weight. Net: positioning and weather point to higher prices over 1-3 months, but price action and this week's storage data argue for near-term softness first.

longTerm.rationale: Weather demand 8-16 days out is +51.7% vs 5y and above the historical max range, price sits below a fair-value curve that itself rises to $3.03 by +21D, and managed-money is net-short at only the 10th percentile of 5 years - a crowded short prone to squeezing higher into Sep, historically the strongest month.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained break below ~$2.65, the demand_8_16D anomaly reverting back inside its normal range, or repeated bearish storage surprises expanding the 5y surplus would invalidate this long thesis.

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk (low conviction, conflicting signals)
shortTerm.rationale: Every technical timeframe (5m-1w) is bearish with 50<200 EMA crosses, and this week's storage build beat forecast by +17 Bcf with inventories +183 Bcf over the 5y average; front price is only marginally below fair (-$0.044) so there is little cushion for a contra-trend long, though near-term (1-7D) weather demand is unremarkable and not a strong headwind either way.
shortTerm.invalidation: A reversal back above $3.00, or the 8-16D heat-demand surge pulling forward into the next week, would flip this bearish view and stop the short out.

keyDrivers: Weather demand_8_16D +51.7% vs 5y and above historical max range, trend rising
COT managed-money net-short at 10th percentile of 5y - crowded short, squeeze risk
Price ~6pp below seasonal median and below a rising fair-value curve
All 6 technical timeframes bearish with 50<200 EMA cross - near-term headwind
Storage +183 Bcf vs 5y surplus with a bearish +17 Bcf surprise this week
risks: Seasonality reliability is explicitly Low (63pp 10-90% spread) - historical odds may not hold this year
Growing storage surplus could cap any weather-driven rally even if demand stays elevated
Near-term bearish technical trend could accelerate before medium-term fundamentals take over, whipsawing the long-term entry
